# Article Types and Outlines

Pick one primary type. Blend only when sections are labeled (e.g. tutorial with a short opinion opener).

## LinkedIn long-form article

**Audience:** Practitioners scrolling feed; mixed seniority  
**Length:** ~800–1,500 words unless platform limit dictates otherwise

```markdown
# [Working title — outcome-oriented]

## Hook (publish as opening lines, not a heading)
(2–4 short lines: tension, mistake, or result — no "In this article I will...")

## Context
(Why now? Who feels this pain?)

## Core insight / thesis
(One paragraph — the single idea worth sharing)

## Section 1 — [Concrete angle]
(narrative + example)

## Section 2 — [How / pattern / lesson]
(code or diagram if helpful)

## Section 3 — [Tradeoffs or failure mode]
(honest limits build trust)

## Takeaways
- Bullet 1 (actionable)
- Bullet 2
- Bullet 3

## Close / CTA
(question for comments, link to repo, or part-2 tease)
```

## Tutorial / how-to

**Audience:** Reader implementing today  
**Purpose:** Reproduce an outcome step by step

```markdown
# [How to X — specific outcome]

## Prerequisites
| Requirement | Version / note |

## What you'll build
(one sentence + optional diagram)

## Steps
### 1. [First verifiable milestone]
### 2. ...
### N. Verify it worked
(expected output or test)

## Troubleshooting
| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |

## Next steps
```

## Opinion / perspective

**Audience:** Peers evaluating a trend or practice  
**Purpose:** Argue a position with evidence

```markdown
# [Claim stated plainly]

## Hook
(controversy or common belief to challenge)

## What people get wrong

## What works instead
(examples, not slogans)

## When the common advice still applies
(fairness improves credibility)

## Summary
```

## Deep-dive / explainer

**Audience:** Reader learning a system or concept  
**Purpose:** Teach mental model

```markdown
# [Concept] — how it actually works

## Hook
(real scenario that needs this concept)

## Mental model
(analogy + diagram)

## Components / layers
(table or diagram)

## Walkthrough
(narrow example traced end-to-end)

## Production considerations
(performance, security, ops — brief)

## Further reading
```

## Announcement / launch

**Audience:** Users or community  
**Purpose:** What shipped, why, how to try

```markdown
# [Product/feature] is available

## What it is (one paragraph)

## Problem it solves

## Highlights
| Feature | Benefit |

## How to get started
(commands or link)

## Limitations / roadmap
(transparency)

## Thank-you / CTA
```
